architecture.summary: XGrammar-2 is a structured generation engine for LLM agents that constrains token sampling to valid grammar states via logit masking. It processes grammars in two phases: preprocessing (Earley-based adaptive token mask cache with JIT compilation amortized over decoding steps) and runtime (per-token mask lookup). The system introduces TagDispatch for intra-request tag-triggered switching between free-form text and structured sub-grammars (using Aho-Corasick automaton for tag matching), and Cross-Grammar Cache for reuse of token masks across different grammars via hierarchical FSM hashing. Repetition state compression bounds grammar size for repetitive patterns. Information flows from grammar → Earley parser → scannable state cache keys → token mask lookup → logit zeroing.

biblio.title: XGrammar-2: Efficient Dynamic Structured Generation Engine for Agentic LLMs

claims.dataset: function calling formats (Llama tool calling, OpenAI Harmony)
claims.statement: XGrammar-2 achieves over 6× faster compilation than prior structured generation engines
XGrammar-2 incurs near-zero end-to-end overhead in modern LLM serving systems

claims.dataset: BFCL tool pool (1908 distinct tools, 100 requests with 500 tools per request)
claims.metric: substructure reuse rate; full structure reuse rate
claims.statement: In dynamic tool-calling workloads with 500 tools, substructure reuse rate reaches 95.6% while full-grammar reuse drops to 0%
XGrammar-2 achieves 7× speedup in end-to-end function calling latency compared to XGrammar
Repetition state compression reduces preprocessing time on JSONSchemaBench by 99.6× over baseline Earley parser

claims.dataset: CONFETTI dataset (Llama tool calling format, OpenAI Harmony Response Format)
claims.statement: XGrammar-2 achieves per-token overhead of approximately 10 ms compilation time versus XGrammar's >1000 ms for dynamic structured generation

TagDispatch introduces a first-class grammar construct for expressing tag-triggered switching between free-form text and structured sub-grammars within a single request, addressing intra-request dynamism more efficiently than plain EBNF encoding

XGrammar-2 with SGLang produces empty outputs for Qwen3-0.6B and language drift (from English to other languages) for Llama3.1-8B when using llguidance-v1.2.0 baseline for dynamic structured generation
